key_insight: The paper proposes a partitioned iterative FEM approach for transient nonlinear eddy-current simulation, solving the field and circuit components separately with Newton iterations and line search.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust and practical simulation of voltage-driven coil systems with nonlinear magnetic materials, such as laminated iron-core devices under transient excitation.
method: Implicit Euler time stepping for transient magnetic vector-potential FEM
Split nonlinear system into a common FE field solve and a coupled circuit equation
Newton’s method applied to each part, with line search for the field nonlinearity
evidence_signal: The authors validate the method on a nonlinear benchmark with a laminated iron core in a cylindrical coil, reporting inrush currents, average flux densities, and numerical performance data.
watch_item: Accuracy and convergence depend on the nonlinear solver coupling and line-search behavior, so implementation details may strongly affect robustness.
paper_title: An Iterative Method for Transient Finite Element Simulations of Non-Linear Eddy Current Problems
